commit.message:
Add 12-month usage stats and UI tweaks

Introduce a calendar-aligned 12-month rolling usage flow and related parsing changes: add getUsageStatsLast12MonthsFlow in ConversationRepository, update extractHistoricalUsage to accept window bounds and fallback date, and compute conversation/message/token totals for the window. Update MenuVM to use the new flow and align the heatmap start to the strict 12-month window (zeroing counts before the window). UI tweaks: MinimalChatInput now computes active modes list and shows a mode icon via ModeIcons when a single mode is active. Fix message selection logic in ChatMessageV2 to prefer the node's current selection when it matches the active version tag and otherwise pick the newest matching message. Assistant pages restyled: group message formatting settings, adjust surfaces, colors and shapes, and improve regex editor layout. Add an MCP picker modal in AssistantToolsSubPage with sync status (including progress indicator) using McpManager.

commit.message:
perf(i18n): optimize batch translation by reusing AI model instance

Reuse the `LanguageModel` instance in `batchTranslate` instead of re-instantiating it for every string.
This reduces the overhead of `translateString` significantly (benchmarked ~1ms per call saved).

- Updated `translateString` to accept optional `model` parameter.
- Updated `batchTranslate` to create model once and pass it to workers.
- Updated imports in `i18n/src/translator.ts`.

Co-authored-by: Cocolalilal <109036699+Cocolalilal@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Suppress auto-scroll and fix activity detection

Detects user scrolling and suppresses automatic scrolling during assistant generation by tracking a userScrolledUp flag (set via snapshotFlow) and only auto-scrolling when the user hasn't scrolled up; auto-scroll now animates to the ScrollBottom spacer and respects canScrollForward. Adjusts deriveActivityState to consider only text after the last tool-related part (hasRecentText) when deciding the Replying state, preventing false "Replying" while tools run. Also removes local/editor config files and build_output.txt.
Fixes to the recent commit

commit.message:
perf: move `deleteChatFiles` to IO dispatcher and fix blocking calls

This change optimizes `deleteChatFiles` in `ChatUtil.kt` by wrapping its execution in `withContext(Dispatchers.IO)` and making it a `suspend` function.

Key changes:
- `ChatUtil.kt`: `deleteChatFiles` is now `suspend` and runs on `Dispatchers.IO`.
- `ChatService.kt`: Updated `checkFilesDelete`, `checkInvalidMessages`, `updateTranslationField`, and `clearTranslationField` to be `suspend` functions. Decoupled file deletion from state updates in `updateConversation` to avoid race conditions.
- `AssistantDetailVM.kt`: Updated `checkAvatarDelete` and `checkBackgroundDelete` to be `suspend`. Refactored `update` function to perform file deletion checks before updating settings store to handle concurrency safely.
- `ChatVM.kt`: Updated `checkUserAvatarDelete` to be `suspend`. Added `deleteFile` helper to launch file deletion in `AppScope`.
- `ChatInput.kt` & `MinimalChatInput.kt`: Refactored `MediaFileInputRow` to accept an `onDelete` callback instead of managing deletion internally. Updated `ChatPage.kt` to pass `vm.deleteFile` to these components, ensuring file deletion survives screen navigation.

This resolves a performance issue where file I/O operations were blocking the calling thread (often the main thread), improving responsiveness. The refactoring also addresses critical race conditions in state management related to file deletion.

Co-authored-by: Cocolalilal <109036699+Cocolalilal@users.noreply.github.com>

Optimize JSON parsing by migrating legacy nodes in database

- Add MIGRATION_22_23 to AppDatabase to migrate legacy 'thinking' nodes to 'reasoning'
- Remove runtime `migrateLegacyNodesJson` check from ConversationRepository
- Clean up unused imports

commit.message:
Add Assistant Material You theming and bump version

Introduce per-assistant Material You theming: add Assistant.useAssistantMaterialYouColors flag and UI toggle (shown when assistant has an avatar or background) in AssistantProfileSubPage. Add AssistantChatTheme implementation to extract a seed color from assistant background/avatar (bitmap loading, palette extraction, sampling) and build a derived color scheme (with AMOLED dark tweak). Wrap ChatPage content with AssistantChatTheme using the current assistant. Also add related string resources and bump app version to 1.3.3 (versionCode 24).

Add background-aware borders to chat UI

Add outline borders to chat input surfaces and make action pills adapt when a background image is present. ChatInput and MinimalChatInput now include a 1dp BorderStroke. NewChatContent gains a hasBackgroundImage flag (default false) which is forwarded to ActionPill instances; ActionPill now chooses its background and border colors based on hasBackgroundImage. ChatPage sets hasBackgroundImage based on currentAssistant.background != null to ensure consistent visuals when using background images.

Use frame-based rotation for avatar loading

Replace spring-based animateTo loop with a frame-driven rotation using withFrameNanos. The code now advances rotation by a fixed 120°/s (one full rotation every 3s) based on frame time deltas, snaps the angle modulo 360, and initializes frame timing. This yields a steady, smooth continuous spin while loading and keeps the previous reset behavior when not loading.

Control regenerate visibility and phantom loading

Make the regenerate action conditional and convert the phantom loading turn into a synthetic assistant group for smoother rendering. Propagate a new showRegenerate flag through ChatList -> ChatMessageTurn -> action components and only render the regenerate icon when appropriate; compute visibility based on the last user message index. Replace the separate phantom loading item with a pending assistant MessageTurnGroup so it can be keyed and morphed into real assistant turns. Also: remove the active-modes tint logic in ChatInput, add haptic handling on assistant bubble clicks, and switch clipboard copying to use message.toContentText().

Improve activity timeline, memory ops & UI tweaks

Add a Chat UI implementation plan and apply multiple UI and backend improvements: return before/after data for memory update/delete in GenerationHandler; add getMemoryById to MemoryRepository; introduce showAssistantBubbles and backgroundDim defaults/mapping in PreferencesStore and Assistant model; adjust avatar sizing and imports in chat components. Refactor ActivityTimelineSheet extensively — convert to LazyColumn, add MemoryAction entries, support edit/delete/restore/revert flows, dialogs, haptics, icons and animated expand/collapse, and wire MemoryRepository for restore/reconciliation. Update ActivityPill click handling to pass activity type. Minor ChatMessageV2 imports and timeline/tool parsing adjustments to support new timeline/memory tooling.

releases.name: LastChat 1.3.7
releases.body:
- Redesigned the recent chats sidepanel.
- Repurposed the "Menu" page to only show stats.
- Added option to choose icons for modes.
- Moved some character settings around.
- Fixed some issues with message editing.
- Fixed the hitbox of the MCP option inside of the "Tools & Search" settings page.

releases.name: LastChat 1.3.6
releases.body:
- Redesigned the top bar in the chat interface.
- Retouched the Menu page.
- Little adjustments to the message input bars and the "Actions" content layout in new chats.
- Removed the Translation page.
- Changed minSDK from 31 to 28.

releases.name: LastChat 1.3.5
releases.body:
This version is mainly here to fix an issue with model proivder keys being deleted at random.

- Restyled the "no models" warning card.
- Made so that by default only OpenRouter is selected as a provider. 
- Added a "saving..." indicator at the top of the model provider editing page.
- Fixed some stuff about the API key encryption.

releases.name: LastChat 1.3.4
releases.body:
- Added in-UI image rendering for the python tool.
- Made the python tool easier to use for the Characters.
- Refined the UI for disabled model providers and MCP servers.
- Made the fullscreen system prompt editor more reliable.
- Added API key encryption.
- Removed firebase analytics for better privacy (kept crash reports).
- Performance optimizations.
- Cleaned up some unused files.

**Attention**: After I released this version, I noticed that some API keys sometimes seem to delete themselves. I'm working on a fix for that!

releases.name: LastChat 1.3.3
releases.body:
This release took quite some time to cook up, I hope the wait was worth it! Here is the changelog:

- The chat interface got a major redesign.
- Added a python tool.
- Wanna change font? Now you can, trough the "Fonts" page.
- Added a slider to make the chat wallpaper darker/lighter if one is selected.
- Refined some UI details for better contrast.
- Added a toggle to use the character's avatar or wallpaper for the color of the chat interface.

readme_html:
LastChat

LastChat is a feature-rich AI assistant application for Android. It is a fork of RikkaHub, modified using Claude 4.5 Opus, GPT Codex and Gemini 3 Pro

This project aims to provide a privacy-focused and highly personalized AI chat experience on Android

Gallery

✨ Key Features

Advanced AI Capabilities

  • Multi-Provider Support: Provider presets make it easier to get up and running. There's support for custom providers too!
  • RAG Memory: Features a RAG-based memory system. Assistants can "remember" details from past conversations using embeddings.
  • Multi-Modal Inputs: Interact using Text, Images, Video, and Audio.

Tools & Integrations

  • Python: Built-in Python Engine (Chaquopy)
  • JavaScript: Built-in JavaScript Engine (QuickJS)
  • Web Search: Integrated web search capabilities to fetch real-time information.
  • MCP: Support for MCP servers.

Assistant Management

  • Multiple Personas: Create, manage, and switch between unlimited custom assistants.
  • Tagging System: Organize assistants with custom tags.
  • Import/Export: Easily share or backup your assistant configurations.

Modern & Fluid UI

  • Material You: The app was designed with Material You 3 Expressive in mind.
  • Rich Rendering: Markdown support with LaTeX for math, code highlighting, and tables.

Additional Modules

  • Image Generation: Dedicated interface for generating images using supported models.
  • Text-to-Speech (TTS): Supports system TTS or other providers.

Privacy & Data

  • Local-First: Chat history and vector memory are stored locally on your device.
  • WebDAV Backup: Securely sync and backup your data to any WebDAV-compatible server.

Built With

  • Kotlin & Jetpack Compose
  • Koin for Dependency Injection
  • Room & DataStore for persistence
  • WorkManager & AlarmManager for reliable background tasks
  • Chaquopy for Python integration
  • QuickJS for JavaScript integration

Credits

  • Original Project: RikkaHub
  • Image cropper is an edited version of the image editor found in LavenderPhotos
  • Made with AI Agents based on:
    • Claude 4.5 Opus
    • GPT Codex
    • Gemini 3 Pro

Note: This project is a fork and may contain modifications or features not present in the original RikkaHub repository.
